keyPoints: Key management is a set of processes used to protect and manage cryptographic keys.
Key lengths are measured in bits and determine the number of possible unique values for a key.
Key lifetimes refer to the period of time a key is considered valid.
Key generation involves creating new keys for use in a cryptographic system.
Key establishment protocols are used to securely share keys between parties.
Key storage refers to the methods used to safely store keys in a system.
Key usage refers to the specific functions a key can perform in a cryptographic system.
Governing key management involves establishing and enforcing policies and procedures for key management.
